blockingIssues.description: web/app/page.tsx hardcodes 24h change to 0, so the token header never shows true positive/negative 24h movement.
API-down flow still surfaces browser console ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED errors, conflicting with the feature's no-console-error expectation.

appliedUpdates.description: Added factual API contract clarifications for /market and /candles to prevent future frontend assumptions about change24h/limit params.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align mission API reference with real contracts: remove `change24h` from `/market` docs (or implement it backend-side), and document current `/candles` query support accurately.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both failed reviews tied implementation confusion to API documentation mismatch: reviewers found `/market` type lacks `change24h` and `/candles` accepts only `interval`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Ensure feature `skillName` values map to actually invokable skills (or add alias mapping) to avoid worker startup failures.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews observed `frontend-ui-worker` in feature metadata but runtime reported skill-not-found before fallback.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add concrete agent-browser command examples for viewport changes and console-error checks to reduce trial-and-error during visual validation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of theme-globals-layout transcript showed repeated unsupported command attempts before successful alternatives were discovered.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce scope boundary that feature commits should avoid unrelated `.factory/` edits unless explicitly requested by mission-level validation tasks.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: token-header-chart review found shared-state file edits in a frontend implementation commit, reducing review isolation.

rejectedObservations.observation: Duplicate skill-resolution mismatch reports across multiple feature reviews.
Hardcoded-color convention drift in layout feature components.
rejectedObservations.reason: already captured as a non-blocking code review issue in feature-level review; no additional shared-state action applied in this synthesis.
